jokes.text: Will anyone back me up if I say this is ridiculous or is it gonna be another avatar situation?
jokes.setup: Jeff wants support for his opinion but references a past group dynamic
jokes.punchline: The implication that the group has previously left him hanging on a controversial opinion about Avatar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's awareness of group dynamics, with efficient storytelling through callback

jokes.text: You mean 'nerd alert.' - Alert nerd.
jokes.setup: Someone says 'spoiler alert' about recording classes
jokes.punchline: The correction to 'nerd alert' followed by Pierce's confused 'alert nerd'
jokes.explanation: Pierce's confusion creates character comedy, very economical delivery

jokes.text: I know the family downstairs hates me. I don't need to understand why.
jokes.setup: Discussing the practical value of learning Spanish
jokes.punchline: Jeff's willful ignorance about his neighbors' hatred is preferable to understanding
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight showing Jeff's avoidance and self-awareness, very quotable

jokes.text: Yeah, you be the cool guy, Jeff. And next fall when that gets lonely, I'll be in the front row of a Spanish 103.
jokes.setup: Jeff is being dismissive about continuing Spanish together
jokes.punchline: Annie's passive-aggressive promise to be in Spanish 103 without him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annie's manipulation tactics and emotional investment

jokes.text: Monday through Friday, 6:00 am. 6:00...
jokes.setup: Annie mentions the Spanish 103 class time
jokes.punchline: Jeff's horrified realization at the early morning schedule
jokes.explanation: Physical reaction comedy showing Jeff's horror at early morning classes

jokes.text: You just 'go girled' yourself. - Shut up. - See, it's fun!
jokes.setup: Shirley says 'go, girl' about men/anthropology
jokes.punchline: Troy pointing out she used the phrase on herself, then celebrating the meta-observation
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about catchphrases, economical and character-driven

jokes.text: To buy a flying car?
jokes.setup: Jeff mentions his unmovable appointment on May 23, 2013
jokes.punchline: Britta's assumption it's something futuristic rather than personal
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke, economical but not particularly deep

jokes.text: It's obvious from your name your parents smoked pot.
jokes.setup: Annie dismissing Britta's casual attitude about friendship
jokes.punchline: The name 'Britta' as evidence of hippie parents
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation that's highly quotable and economical

jokes.text: Hola, el losers.
jokes.setup: Chang entering and greeting the group in Spanish
jokes.punchline: Mixing Spanish greeting with English insult using Spanish article
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang wordplay showing his casual cruelty and bad Spanish

jokes.text: Other side of the door.
jokes.setup: Chang asks Pierce to close the door from inside
jokes.punchline: Clarification that Pierce should be outside when he closes it
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy beat, well-executed and economical

jokes.text: I prefer the term 'better than real,' but yes.
jokes.setup: Chang asks if Jeff's degree was fake
jokes.punchline: Jeff's euphemistic reframing of his fraudulent degree
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff Winger line showing his ability to spin anything positively

jokes.text: Two questions. Where did you get it, could you have prevented being caught, and how? One question. Where did you learn to count questions and are you telling me you need a fake degree?
jokes.setup: Chang says 'two questions' then asks three, Jeff says 'one question' then asks two
jokes.punchline: Both characters failing at basic counting while discussing academic fraud
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical comedy that reflects both characters' educational inadequacies

jokes.text: Did you say 'key-tar' or did you just pronounce guitar like a hillbilly? Key-tar. It's a keyboard you can play like a guitar. Get with the times, man.
jokes.setup: Chang mentions being in a band with a key-tar
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confusion about the instrument and Chang's defensive explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chang's delusions and Jeff's bewilderment

jokes.text: I'm supposed to tip my bag boy?
jokes.setup: Chang mentions bagging groceries for 5 bucks an hour plus tips
jokes.punchline: Jeff's shocked realization he should be tipping grocery baggers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff reaction showing his privileged obliviousness

jokes.text: I faked my way into a job as a Spanish teacher at a community college relying on phrases from sesame street.
jokes.setup: Chang explaining his career path after grocery bagging
jokes.punchline: Using children's TV show Spanish to teach college students
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd revelation that explains Chang's terrible Spanish teaching

jokes.text: That was the chair. Good. Good.
jokes.setup: Mysterious chair noise during tense conversation
jokes.punchline: Chang's nervous explanation that it was just furniture
jokes.explanation: Mysterious running gag that becomes funnier in retrospect

jokes.text: If the Dean finds out that I'm not a real teacher, your grades are invalid, and you'll all have to repeat this class. That was the chair.
jokes.setup: Jeff repeating Chang's threat from memory
jokes.punchline: Adding the mysterious chair comment at the end
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds the running gag effectively

jokes.text: Um, he's not faking his death again, is he? Because I can't handle that roller coaster. First he's alive. And then we're happy. And then he's alive again and...
jokes.setup: Dean announces Chang is 'no longer with us'
jokes.punchline: Britta's traumatic memory of Chang's previous fake death
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing lasting trauma from Chang's antics

jokes.text: Word of advice, if an Asian man says he's a Spanish teacher, it's not racist to ask for proof, okay? You ignore your mother's voice and get right into that horse's mouth.
jokes.setup: Dean explaining how Chang was caught as a fraud
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly phrased advice about racial profiling and mixed metaphor
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about racial assumptions delivered in Dean's awkward style

jokes.text: Listen to this back and forth. It's like I'm at an embassy.
jokes.setup: Dr. Escodera speaking rapid Spanish with students
jokes.punchline: Pierce comparing basic Spanish class to diplomatic setting
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce observation showing his confusion with foreign language

jokes.text: She wants a large coffee.
jokes.setup: Dr. Escodera says 'Veinte' (twenty)
jokes.punchline: Pierce thinks it's a coffee order (venti)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Pierce's Starbucks-centric worldview

jokes.text: El crappo.
jokes.setup: Learning the exam covers the entire textbook
jokes.punchline: Jeff's fake Spanish expletive
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay, economical but not particularly sophisticated

jokes.text: And why is she teaching Spanish if she's a doctor? Go cure something.
jokes.setup: Confusion about Dr. Escodera being both doctor and Spanish teacher
jokes.punchline: Troy's imperative that doctors should prioritize medical work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic and delivery, highly quotable

jokes.text: I know. Yours is a 2002. That's the year I heard it.
jokes.setup: Troy identifying Jeff's car alarm sound from fireworks
jokes.punchline: Claiming to distinguish car alarm sounds by model year
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd Troy moment showing his strange expertise areas

jokes.text: You're wearing protective goggles to destroy my car? Safety first!
jokes.setup: Jeff catching Chang vandalizing his car
jokes.punchline: Chang prioritizing safety while committing crimes
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment showing Chang's twisted priorities

jokes.text: Oh, oh, uh, we're writing a song. For the battle of bands.
jokes.setup: Chang caught in violent confrontation with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Absurd explanation that they're collaborating musically
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Chang's desperate lie

jokes.text: At one point, I was teaching you Klingon! Yeah? Why don't you tell that to your really weird sounding magical chair? I have a condition!
jokes.setup: Chang defending his teaching methods
jokes.punchline: Revelation that the mysterious chair sounds are Chang's medical condition
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to running gag, absurd and perfectly Chang

jokes.text: You could be a plumber.
jokes.setup: Plumber's passionate recruitment speech
jokes.punchline: Troy's contemplative repetition showing he's genuinely considering it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's impressionability

jokes.text: Maybe it's one of those recessive genes. I can also curl my tongue. Who else?
jokes.setup: Someone's 'hmm' reaction being suspicious
jokes.punchline: Pierce connecting it to genetics and demonstrating tongue curling
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce non-sequitur showing his confused logic

jokes.text: Because you didn't get hot until after High School.
jokes.setup: Jeff analyzing Annie's motivations for betraying Chang
jokes.punchline: Brutal personal attack about her late physical development
jokes.explanation: Savage Jeff line that's psychologically astute and cruel

jokes.text: 'That's true.'
jokes.setup: Jeff's brutal assessment
jokes.punchline: Annie's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction. Annie's simple acknowledgment deflates Jeff's cruelty and shows her self-awareness. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Someone make her a dude so I can punch her.
jokes.setup: Group anger at Annie for betraying them
jokes.punchline: Troy's solution involving gender transformation to enable violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy logic showing his moral confusion about violence

jokes.text: 'Someone make her a dude so I can punch her.'
jokes.setup: Shirley's anger at Annie
jokes.punchline: Her frustration with social constraints on violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Shirley moment. Her Christian morality clashing with violent impulses. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Now she is going to make the Disney face. Her lip is gonna quiver and her eyes will flutter, but they won't ever actually close.
jokes.setup: Annie about to cry from group criticism
jokes.punchline: Abed's clinical prediction of her emotional manipulation tactics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-observation that's incredibly specific and accurate

jokes.text: She's the ark of the covenant!
jokes.setup: Jeff telling everyone to close their eyes to avoid Annie's manipulation
jokes.punchline: Comparing Annie's emotional power to the deadly biblical artifact
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference that perfectly captures the situation

jokes.text: Picture her as Paul Giamatti!
jokes.setup: Trying to help the group resist Annie's emotional manipulation
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they imagine her as an unattractive middle-aged actor
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference that's mean but funny

jokes.text: Did the sisterhood of the traveling pants poison each other's food so they were too sick to leave? No! I've never seen it, but I'm pretty sure they mailed each other pants!
jokes.setup: Comparing Annie's betrayal to how friends should behave
jokes.punchline: Jeff's passionate analysis of a movie he admits not seeing
jokes.explanation: Fantastic escalating rant showing Jeff's passionate confusion

jokes.text: That's what my rehab group used to say. The only one that keeps in touch is the guy that sells jewelry made out of bottle caps.
jokes.setup: Discussion about friends staying in touch after groups end
jokes.punchline: Britta's depressing rehab experience with oddly specific detail
jokes.explanation: Great dark Britta moment with perfect specific detail

jokes.text: Here's a b-side from that album, antelope slaughter at Ndutu.
jokes.setup: Dean continuing 'soothing' nature sounds policy
jokes.punchline: Violent animal death sounds as stress relief
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about the Dean's obliviousness to context

jokes.text: You have to know how to do it. Discrimination lawsuit.
jokes.setup: Group needs to wake up Pierce
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately thinks of legal liability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff - sees legal problems in simple situations due to his background

jokes.text: Troy, you know what the best part of my day is? For about 10 seconds from the time I sit down in Spanish to the time I look at your desk, 'cause I think 'maybe I'll look and he won't be there.'
jokes.setup: Jeff giving Troy relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Cruel adaptation of romantic movie dialogue about hoping Troy disappears
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cruel twist on famous movie speech

jokes.text: It's from a movie. Well, then the movie it's from is terrible.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining Jeff's cruel speech was from a movie
jokes.punchline: Troy condemning the movie without understanding the reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's innocent reaction

jokes.text: uh, I'm friends with Hannah too. The whole class is. And you can't have an exam without a class. Right, guys? We love Hannah! We love Hannah!
jokes.setup: Annie trying to convince Spanish class not to take exam
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to rally classmates who barely know her
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Annie's desperation and delusion

jokes.text: Oh, no! The devil told the truth!
jokes.setup: Pierce's horoscope prediction about suffering coming true
jokes.punchline: Shirley's religious interpretation of astrology accuracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley line combining her religious views with superstition

jokes.text: I was showing Annie the magic of electronic sampling. No--no--no--no-- no--no--nooooooo!
jokes.setup: Group thinks Chang was torturing Annie based on screaming
jokes.punchline: Chang claiming it was music education, followed by more 'sampling'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Chang's twisted creativity

jokes.text: And you don't have to dress like this to grow up. You--you look like a travel Agent.
jokes.setup: Jeff tells Annie she doesn't need to change to mature
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults her new professional outfit
jokes.explanation: Jeff's attempt at sincerity undermined by his inability to not critique

jokes.text: You--you look like a travel Agent.
jokes.setup: Annie asking about her new 'mature' outfit
jokes.punchline: Jeff's specific and deflating comparison
jokes.explanation: Good specific insult that deflates Annie's pretensions

jokes.text: Uh, like the first season of the wire.
jokes.setup: Troy saying he got half the Spanish exam which helped with the other half
jokes.punchline: Comparing Spanish test comprehension to complex TV show
jokes.explanation: Clever Troy comparison showing his cultural awareness

jokes.text: Hey, thanks... For making it easy. I was going to say the same to you... But you were gone before breakfast. I'm an early riser. I'll say.
jokes.setup: Seemingly innocent conversation between Jeff and the Spanish professor
jokes.punchline: Heavy implication they slept together and she made the test easy in return
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff ending showing his manipulation and the sexual subtext
